description: Zero-friction session-based ranking and matchmaking app for local community sports (Pickleball and Cornhole). Host creates a session, players join via QR, app suggests balanced 2v2 matches using Elo ratings, and updates a live leaderboard in real-time.

userStories.title: Database schema and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Supabase schema set up so all features have a working data layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `sessions` table: id (uuid PK), slug (text unique), sport (text), status (text: 'active'|'ended', default 'active'), created_at (timestamp)
Create `players` table: id (uuid PK), session_id (uuid FK -> sessions), name (text), elo (int default 1000), status (text: 'available'|'playing'|'resting'), is_active (bool default true)
Create `matches` table: id (uuid PK), session_id (uuid FK -> sessions), player_a1, player_a2, player_b1, player_b2 (uuid FKs -> players), score_a (int), score_b (int), elo_change (float)
Add unique constraint on (session_id, name) in players table
Add RLS policies: public read/write on all three tables, no auth required
Enable Supabase Realtime on players and matches tables
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Supabase project: ceyzbfgoayjyxhweywtu. Tables created with RLS and Realtime enabled on players and matches.

userStories.title: Slug generation utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility that generates unique human-readable slugs so session URLs are memorable and typeable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lib/slug.ts that exports generateSlug(): Promise<string>
Slugs are in adjective-noun format (e.g., fuzzy-panda, swift-eagle) using embedded wordlists
Function queries Supabase to check for slug collision and retries up to 5 times before throwing
Generated slugs are lowercase and URL-safe with no spaces
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created src/lib/slug.ts with adjective-noun wordlists, Supabase collision check, and up to 5 retry attempts.

userStories.title: Elo engine utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure utility module that calculates Elo changes so the ranking logic is testable and reusable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lib/elo.ts that exports calculateEloChange(matchInput): EloResult
matchInput includes: teamA player Elos, teamB player Elos, score_a, score_b
Team rating = average of the two player Elos
Expected outcome: E_A = 1 / (1 + 10^((R_B - R_A) / 400))
MOV multiplier: M = ln(|score_A - score_B| + 1) * 2.2 / ((R_winner - R_loser) * 0.001 + 2.2)
New Elo = Old Elo + 32 * (S - E) * M where S=1 for win, S=0 for loss
EloResult returns new Elo for all 4 players and the elo_change magnitude
Function is pure — no side effects, no database calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created src/lib/elo.ts with pure calculateEloChange(MatchInput): EloResult. Team avg Elo, expected outcome, MOV multiplier, K=32. Denominator clamped to 0.1 to avoid division by zero for large upsets.

userStories.title: Create session — home page and server action
userStories.description: As a host, I want to create a new game session so that players can join and we can start playing.
acceptanceCriteria: Home page at / shows a 'Create Session' form with a sport selector (Pickleball or Cornhole) and a Submit button
On submit, a server action calls generateSlug() and inserts a row into sessions with sport and generated slug
Host is redirected to /session/[slug]/host after creation
The host URL serves as the secret management link — no login or PIN
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/actions.ts with createSession server action (generateSlug + insert + redirect). Updated src/app/page.tsx with sport selector form.

userStories.title: Host dashboard — QR code display
userStories.description: As a host, I want to display a QR code so players can join the session from their phones.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /session/[slug]/host renders a host dashboard
Dashboard displays a large QR code generated with react-qr-code pointing to /session/[slug]/join
Session slug and sport name are displayed as text alongside the QR as a manual fallback
Page returns 404 if the slug does not exist in sessions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/session/[slug]/host/page.tsx (server component: fetches session, 404 on missing slug, builds join URL from request headers) and QRDisplay.tsx (client component wrapping react-qr-code). Build passes.

userStories.title: Player join page
userStories.description: As a player, I want to scan the QR code and enter my nickname so I can join the session.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /session/[slug]/join shows a nickname text input and a Join button
If session status is 'ended', show a friendly message and hide the join form
On submit, insert a player row with name, session_id, status='available', elo=1000
If nickname is already taken in this session, show an inline error message
On success, redirect player to /session/[slug]/player/[player_id]
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/session/[slug]/join/page.tsx (server component: fetches session, 404 if missing, ended message if status='ended') and JoinForm.tsx (client component using useActionState with joinSession server action). Added joinSession to actions.ts: checks duplicate nickname, inserts player row, redirects to /session/[slug]/player/[id]. Build and typecheck pass.

userStories.title: Player view — availability toggle
userStories.description: As a player, I want to view my status and toggle my availability so the matchmaker knows if I want to play next.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /session/[slug]/player/[id] shows the player's nickname, current Elo, and current status
Page shows a toggle button labeled 'Available' or 'Resting' reflecting current status
Clicking the button updates player status in Supabase (available <-> resting)
Button is disabled (not clickable) when player status is 'playing'
Button color: green when available, gray when resting
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/session/[slug]/player/[id]/page.tsx (server component: fetches player + validates session slug, notFound on mismatch) and StatusToggle.tsx (client component: useTransition + router.refresh for optimistic toggle). Added togglePlayerStatus server action to actions.ts. Build and typecheck pass.

userStories.title: Host dashboard — live player list with Realtime
userStories.description: As a host, I want to see all players and their current status in real-time so I can manage the session.
acceptanceCriteria: Host dashboard lists all active players (is_active=true) with name, Elo, and status badge
Status badges: green=available, blue=playing, gray=resting
List subscribes to Supabase Realtime on players table and updates without page refresh
Each player row has a 'Remove' button that sets is_active=false (hides player from pool)
Each player's Elo is editable inline — clicking it shows an integer input that saves on Enter or blur
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/session/[slug]/host/PlayerList.tsx (client component: Supabase Realtime subscription, inline Elo editing, Remove button). Added removePlayer and updatePlayerElo server actions to actions.ts. Updated host page.tsx to fetch initial players and pass to PlayerList. Build and typecheck pass.

userStories.title: Host dashboard — match suggestion
userStories.description: As a host, I want the app to suggest the most balanced 2v2 match so games are fair and competitive.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Suggest Match' button is disabled when fewer than 4 players have status='available'
Clicking the button runs the suggestion algorithm: evaluates all C(n,4)*3 team splits of available players, picks the split with the smallest win probability delta using team average Elo
Suggested match is displayed: Team A (Player1 & Player2) vs Team B (Player1 & Player2) with win % shown for each team
Host can click 'Accept' to insert a match row and set all 4 players to status='playing'
Host can click 'Re-shuffle' to run the algorithm again and show a new suggestion
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added match suggestion UI to PlayerList.tsx: generateAllSuggestions() evaluates all C(n,4)*3 team splits sorted by win% delta. State-driven suggestion display with Re-shuffle (cycles through sorted candidates) and Accept (calls acceptMatch server action: inserts match row + sets 4 players to 'playing'). Suggest Match button disabled when <4 available players.

userStories.title: Host dashboard — score entry and Elo update
userStories.description: As a host, I want to enter the final score of a match so Elo ratings update and the leaderboard reflects results.
acceptanceCriteria: Active matches (players with status='playing') are listed on host dashboard with score inputs for Team A and Team B
Host enters integer scores and clicks 'Submit Score'
Server action calls calculateEloChange() from lib/elo.ts with the 4 player Elos and scores
Match row is updated with score_a, score_b, and elo_change
All 4 players' elo values are updated in the players table
All 4 players' statuses revert to 'available' after score submission
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added submitScore server action to actions.ts: fetches match + 4 players, calls calculateEloChange(), updates match (score_a, score_b, elo_change), updates each player's elo, sets all 4 statuses to 'available'. Host page now fetches initial active matches (score_a IS NULL) and passes as initialMatches to PlayerList. PlayerList: added matches state, Realtime subscription on matches table, scoreInputs state per match, Active Matches section with Team A/B player names, score inputs, and Submit Score button.

userStories.title: Live leaderboard page (public, slug-gated)
userStories.description: As a player or spectator, I want to view a live leaderboard so I can see rankings at any time.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /session/[slug] shows a leaderboard — no join required, accessible to anyone with the slug
Leaderboard shows rank, nickname, Elo, and status badge for each active player (is_active=true), sorted by Elo descending
Page subscribes to Supabase Realtime on players table — updates without refresh when Elo or status changes
If session status is 'ended', show a 'Session Ended' banner above the final standings
Player view and host dashboard both link to or embed this leaderboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created src/app/session/[slug]/page.tsx (server component: fetches session + active players, notFound on missing slug) and src/app/session/[slug]/Leaderboard.tsx (client component: Realtime subscription on players table, sorted by Elo desc, Session Ended banner when status=ended). Added 'View Leaderboard →' link to host dashboard and player view pages.

userStories.title: End session and archived summary
userStories.description: As a host, I want to end the session and produce a read-only summary so final standings are preserved.
acceptanceCriteria: Host dashboard shows an 'End Session' button
Clicking it opens a confirmation Dialog (Shadcn Dialog component)
If any matches are active (players with status='playing'), show a warning in the dialog before allowing confirmation
On confirm, update session status to 'ended' in Supabase
After ending, /session/[slug] shows the leaderboard in read-only archived mode with a 'Session Ended' banner
No new players can join an ended session (join page shows friendly message)
Host dashboard shows a permanent 'Session Ended' banner and disables Suggest Match and End Session buttons
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added endSession server action to actions.ts (updates session status to 'ended'). Created EndSessionButton.tsx (client component using @radix-ui/react-dialog: confirms end session, warns if active matches present, calls endSession + router.refresh()). Updated host/page.tsx to show 'Session Ended' banner when status=ended, pass sessionEnded prop to PlayerList, and render EndSessionButton. Updated PlayerList.tsx to accept sessionEnded prop and disable Suggest Match when session is ended.
